verses.text: Jethro, Moses' father-in-law and priest of Midian, heard all God had done for Moses and Israel. He took Zipporah, Moses' wife — whom Moses had sent away — and her two sons: Gershom, meaning sojourner, and Eliezer, meaning my God is helper. Guidance: Even the deliverer had a family he had to send away during the work. Jethro brings them back. God restores what costly seasons of obedience cost us.

Guidance: Even the deliverer had a family he had to send away during the work. Jethro brings them back. God restores what costly seasons of obedience cost us.

He took Zipporah, Moses' wife — whom Moses had sent away — and her two sons: Gershom, meaning sojourner, and Eliezer, meaning my God is helper. Guidance: Even the deliverer had a family he had to send away during the work. Jethro brings them back. God restores what costly seasons of obedience cost us.

Jethro, Moses' father-in-law and priest of Midian, heard all God had done for Moses and Israel. He took Zipporah, Moses' wife — whom Moses had sent away — and her two sons: Gershom, meaning sojourner, and Eliezer, meaning my God is helper. Guidance: Even the deliverer had a family he had to send away during the work. Jethro brings them back. God restores what costly seasons of obedience cost us.

Jethro came with Moses' sons and wife to him in the wilderness, where he camped at the mount of God. He sent word to Moses: I, your father-in-law Jethro, am come to you, and your wife, and her two sons with her. Guidance: Family reunions in ministry need to be planned and announced. Jethro sends ahead rather than surprising Moses mid-work.

Jethro came with Moses' sons and wife to him in the wilderness, where he camped at the mount of God. He sent word to Moses: I, your father-in-law Jethro, am come to you, and your wife, and her two sons with her. Guidance: Family reunions in ministry need to be planned and announced. Jethro sends ahead rather than surprising Moses mid-work.

Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, did obeisance, kissed him; they came into the tent. Moses told Jethro all the LORD had done to Pharaoh for Israel's sake, all the hardship in the way, and how the LORD delivered them. Guidance: Even a leader bows before his father-in-law. Then Moses tells the whole story — not as a complaint, but as a testimony.

Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, did obeisance, kissed him; they came into the tent. Moses told Jethro all the LORD had done to Pharaoh for Israel's sake, all the hardship in the way, and how the LORD delivered them. Guidance: Even a leader bows before his father-in-law. Then Moses tells the whole story — not as a complaint, but as a testimony.

Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness the LORD had done to Israel. He said: blessed be the LORD, who delivered you from Egypt and Pharaoh. Now I know that the LORD is greater than all gods — for in the thing they dealt proudly, He was above them. Guidance: A Midianite priest hears one testimony and confesses the LORD's supremacy. Your story may convert someone you weren't trying to convert. Tell it.

Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness the LORD had done to Israel. He said: blessed be the LORD, who delivered you from Egypt and Pharaoh. Now I know that the LORD is greater than all gods — for in the thing they dealt proudly, He was above them. Guidance: A Midianite priest hears one testimony and confesses the LORD's supremacy. Your story may convert someone you weren't trying to convert. Tell it.

Jethro took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God; Aaron and all the elders of Israel came to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before God. Guidance: Worship and shared meals braid together here. A father-in-law from outside the covenant family sits at the table with the priest of Israel. God's table has wider room than we think.

Next day Moses sat to judge the people; they stood from morning till evening. Jethro asked: what is this you do? Why sit alone while all stand from morning till evening? Moses: the people come to enquire of God; when they have a matter, I judge and teach them God's statutes. Guidance: A leader trapped in the right activity can still be in the wrong system. Don't confuse busyness with sustainability.

Moses: the people come to enquire of God; when they have a matter, I judge and teach them God's statutes. Guidance: A leader trapped in the right activity can still be in the wrong system. Don't confuse busyness with sustainability.

Jethro: the thing you do is not good. You will surely wear away — both you and this people with you. This thing is too heavy for you; you are not able to perform it alone. Guidance: Wise advice from outside often sees what loyal insiders cannot say. Listen to the in-laws and mentors who name your unsustainable patterns.

Jethro: the thing you do is not good. You will surely wear away — both you and this people with you. This thing is too heavy for you; you are not able to perform it alone. Guidance: Wise advice from outside often sees what loyal insiders cannot say. Listen to the in-laws and mentors who name your unsustainable patterns.

Hearken to my voice. Be for the people Godward, bringing their causes to God. Teach them His laws and ways. Choose out of all the people able men who fear God, love truth, and hate covetousness — make them rulers of thousands, hundreds, fifties, tens. Let them judge at all seasons; bring only great matters to you. Guidance: Delegation is spiritual. Find people who fear God, love truth, and hate gain — then trust them with what they can carry.

Choose out of all the people able men who fear God, love truth, and hate covetousness — make them rulers of thousands, hundreds, fifties, tens. If you do this thing, and God commands you so, then you shall be able to endure, and all this people shall also go to their place in peace. Guidance: Healthy structure protects both the leader and the people. The goal is endurance, not heroics. The whole community arrives at peace when one person stops carrying it all.

Moses listened to the voice of his father-in-law and did all he said. He chose able men out of all Israel and made them rulers of thousands, hundreds, fifties, tens. They judged at all seasons; hard cases came to Moses, small matters they handled. Guidance: Moses listened. The deliverer of nations heard correction from his wife's father. Humility in leadership often means letting outsiders redesign your week.

They judged at all seasons; hard cases came to Moses, small matters they handled. Guidance: Moses listened. The deliverer of nations heard correction from his wife's father. Humility in leadership often means letting outsiders redesign your week.

And Moses let his father-in-law depart; he went his way into his own land. Guidance: Mentors come and go. Receive the wisdom, then let them return home. Not every helpful voice needs to stay forever to have shaped you.
